channel.title: Posts on I'm Keith Kee KW
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on I'm Keith Kee KW

item.title: How to Set Up SSH Public Key Authentication Windows 10
item.description: Launch the Command Prompt and proceed with the command below: ssh-keygen Ignore the passphrase by simply hit the Enter key. After generating both the private and public keys, proceed to login to the server via SSH using the username & password. Once you are in the server, proceed to create a folder as shown below: sudo mkdir -p /.ssh Create a file with name of authorized_keys:

item.title: Basic Git Commands for Beginner
item.description: git clone The function of the git clone to save a copy of the main branch from Github into the local hard drive. Below is the example on how to use this command: git clone https://github.com/keithkeekw/test_repo.git Below are the example: git status It is to get the status of the working files. If there’s any changes on any files, it will list it accordingly.

item.title: How to Use Crontab
item.description: Cron is a tool to configure scheduled tasks in Unix/Linux system. It is like Task Scheduler in Windows system. crontab is a command to edit all the scheduled tasks in the machine. Cron Table Format * * * * * Command_to_execute | | | | | | | | | Day of the Week ( 0 - 6 ) ( Sunday = 0 ) | | | | | | | Month ( 1 - 12 ) | | | | | Day of Month ( 1 - 31 ) | | | Hour ( 0 - 23 ) | Min ( 0 - 59 ) Specifying multiple values in a field

item.title: How to Set Up Samba Server in Raspberry Pi
item.description: Ensure the Raspberry Pi’s repositories are up-to-date by performing the command below in the terminal: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade -y Next, proceed to install all the required packages from the remote repositories for Samba by running the command below: sudo apt-get install samba samba-common-bin After the installation has been completed, we can proceed to create a folder to be shared via the Samba Server.

item.title: Book Review: Make Time
item.description: Authors: Jake Knapp & John Zeratsky Type: Non-Fiction Genre: Productivity Links: Goodread.com | Amazon.com Summary All of us are have 24 hours a day to live our life and most of us will complain about insufficient time to actually live in the way we wanted and to pursue activity or experience we truly enjoy. Make Time offers an easy framework to carve out some time of our day to do things or tasks that matter the most to us and the framework only consists of 4 elements.

item.title: Installation of PostgreSQL in Raspberry Pi
item.description: Installation Run the following command to install the packages: sudo apt install postgresql -y When that’s complete, postsgre user account will be automatically created in the user account. Before proceed further give the postgre account a password. Run the command as below: sudo passwd postgres Next, change the user account from pi to postgres.: su postgres Proceed to login to the database via psql command and you will be able to access to the database without any password since the user postgres does not have any password assigned to it by default.

item.title: The Zen of Python
item.description: import this Beautiful is better than ugly. Explicit is better than implicit. Simple is better than complex. Complex is better than complicated. Flat is better than nested. Sparse is better than dense. Readability counts. Special cases aren’t special enough to break the rules. Although practicality beats purity. Errors should never pass silently. Unless explicitly silenced. In the face of ambiguity, refuse the temptation to guess. There should be one– and preferably only one –obvious way to do it.
